sourceScope.primarySourceFile: AG 31 - Valuation Issues vs Policy Form Approval.pdf
sourceScope.sourceTitle: AG 31 - Valuation Issues vs Policy Form Approval

observedSectionWindows.note: Policy-form approval caveat, annual-statement reserve context, and the closing boundary note.

boundaries: Keep planning focused on this single AG 31 PDF.
Do not widen into AG 32 or later guideline files.
Keep the policy-form / approval / reserve context explicit in every batch artifact.
Keep the page image as the backstop for exact wording because the page contains encoding noise.
Treat the guideline as review-only regulatory guidance, not learner-facing policy text.
Keep the batch source-bound and review-only.
exclusions: No eLearning scripts, slides, quizzes, or app exports.
No raw source files in Git.
No other guideline files in the same wave.

topicMap.label: Policy-form approval caveat
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the one-page window together and do not widen into AG 32.

proposedBatches.title: AG 31 policy-form approval caveat slice

pageTarget.notes: Capture the full one-page guideline as one review-only slice because the approval caveat and annual-statement reserve context belong together.

proposedBatches.rationale: Capture the full one-page guideline as one review-only slice because the approval caveat and annual-statement reserve context belong together.

reviewStandards.regulatoryRequirement: Treat the approval-caveat and reserve-language as regulatory text when the guideline directs how the reserve issue should be understood.
reviewStandards.caveatOrCompanionGuidance: Treat the policy-form approval caveat as review-only guidance that should stay separate from any endorsement claim.
reviewStandards.definitionOrTerminology: Treat policy form, benefit, reserve issue, annual statement, and endorsement references as terminology or context when the text explains rather than directs.
reviewStandards.crossReferenceMapping: Treat references to annual statements, approval status, and appropriate reserves as cross-reference context unless the text operationalizes them.
reviewStandards.backgroundContent: Treat the title, scene-setting paragraph, and explanatory transition language as background unless they impose a requirement.
reviewStandards.boundaryControlWindow: Treat the page 1 window as a review-only slice and do not split it unless a later cleanup pass explicitly needs that split.
reviewStandards.requiresHumanInterpretation: Treat the encoded page, approval caveat, and annual-statement reserve examples as requiring human review unless the source text is explicit.

appReady: export is sanitized, stable, and versioned
ragReady: review status no longer requires human disposition

currentChecksNeeded: Plan file exists and parses as JSON.
The AG 31 plan markdown exists and carries the expected headings.
Every proposed batch remains review-only by default.
The source posture is explicitly active.
Each planned batch has a matching batch definition.
The AG 31 batch definition file stays synchronized with the plan.
futureChecksSuggested: No learner-facing, app-ready, or RAG-ready promotion checks.
scriptImplications: scripts/ag31-batch-definitions.mjs should stay synchronized with the planned batch-113 slice when extraction is authorized.
scripts/batch-definitions.mjs should import the AG 31 definition file so the shared runner can find batch-113.
scripts/validate-scaffold.mjs should verify that the AG 31 plan file exists, parses cleanly, and preserves the review-only posture.
The shared runner should continue to honor the same stop conditions used for the earlier guideline batches.

notes: Controlled AG 31 planning artifact. Planned batch 113 remains review-only by default and is not an extraction run.
